key_strengths: Portfolio of ~28 niche vertical software businesses with 80%+ recurring revenue and 95%+ gross retention
Exceptional cash economics: 69% gross margins, 31% FCF margins, 158% FCF conversion, 1.3% capex intensity
Deep switching costs — system-of-record software in regulated verticals where migration cost dwarfs license fees
20-year track record of transformative capital allocation deploying ~$25B in acquisitions
Trading at half its 5-year average P/E with price below bear-case IV across all modeled scenarios
key_risks: AI-native startups targeting Roper's verticals (legal, insurance, healthcare) could gradually unbundle vertical software moats
Debt/EBITDA risen from 2.4x to 3.0x in two years with interest coverage declining from 11.6x to 7.0x
Concurrent cyclical headwinds in Deltek (GovCon), Neptune (water meters), and DAT (freight)
Acquisition execution risk at scale — $6B+ deployment capacity means a single bad deal could impair 15-20% of EV
Low insider ownership (~0.4-3%) limits management's personal downside alignment
red_flags: RBC downgraded ROP in early 2026 citing AI disruption uncertainty
Interest expense nearly doubled from $165M to $325M in two years
Reported ROIC of 6.4% below WACC of 8.2% (distorted by goodwill but still a metric to monitor)
Tangible book value is deeply negative — entire equity base is goodwill and intangibles
Stock bottomed only 5 weeks ago at $313; bottoming process may not be complete
buy_triggers: Organic growth recovers above 7% as Deltek/Neptune/DAT headwinds fade, confirming cyclical not structural deceleration
Well-executed acquisition deploys $2B+ of $6B capacity at reasonable multiples, demonstrating continued discipline
Net revenue retention holds above 105% for two consecutive quarters, disproving AI disruption narrative
sell_triggers: Net revenue retention drops below 100% for two consecutive years, signaling AI-driven moat erosion
Organic growth turns negative for two consecutive years, indicating structural impairment
Debt/EBITDA exceeds 4.0x with no credible deleveraging plan, or a failed large acquisition destroys credibility
valuation_summary: At $354 (15.2x forward P/E vs 5-year average of 32x, 14.6x EV/EBITDA vs 10-year median of 22x), the stock trades below the quant bear-case IV of $411 and below every cell in the 25-scenario sensitivity grid (floor $583). Monte Carlo P(IV > Price) = 100% across 10,000 simulations. The market is pricing in permanent 2-3% organic growth and zero accretive M&A — a structural impairment thesis that contradicts the 80% recurring revenue base and $6B deployment capacity.
compact_checklist: Roper owns ~28 niche vertical software businesses (legal, healthcare, freight, government) that serve as the operational backbone of their customers' daily workflows, producing 80%+ recurring revenue with 95%+ gross retention.

Switching costs are enormous because replacing system-of-record software (court management, healthcare compliance, freight logistics) requires 6-12 months of disruption, data migration, and retraining — confirmed by 95%+ retention and stable 69% gross margins for four consecutive years.

FCF of $2.4B on $7.9B revenue (31% margin) with only $105M capex (1.3% of sales) and 158% FCF-to-net-income conversion makes this one of the most capital-efficient large-cap businesses in the S&P 500.

A 20-year track record of deploying ~$25B in acquisitions that transformed a cyclical industrial into an 80% recurring-revenue software portfolio, with 96% at-risk CEO compensation, though insider ownership at ~0.4-3% is disappointingly low.

Debt/EBITDA of 3.0x with $9.3B total debt is serviceable given $2.4B annual FCF and well-laddered maturities, but interest coverage has deteriorated from 11.6x to 7.0x in two years due to acquisition-funded borrowing.

At $354 (15x forward earnings vs 5-year average of 32x), the market is pricing in permanent 2-3% organic growth and zero accretive M&A — a structural impairment thesis that contradicts the 80% recurring revenue base and $6B deployment capacity.

The stock trades 14% below the quant bear-case IV of $411 and below every cell in the 25-scenario sensitivity grid (floor $583), with a 4:1 upside/downside ratio and near-zero permanent capital loss risk across 28 diversified software businesses.

Sell if net revenue retention drops below 100% for two consecutive years, signaling that AI-native competitors are successfully unbundling Roper's vertical software moats and eroding the switching-cost architecture that underpins the entire thesis.
source_summary: Roper Technologies 2025 10-K, Q4 2025 earnings release and call transcript, 2024 DEF 14A proxy, Yahoo Finance (yfinance auto-fetch), quant-valuation model (DCF/MC/sensitivity), Seeking Alpha, Investing.com, TIKR, RBC Capital research, PitchGrade Research, MatrixBCG, BeyondSPX, ropertech.com IR filings
